spec_match.rationale: Three lanes (same-origin / same-site / synthetic cross-site) explicitly surface the partition key the v137 change enforces.

interactive.rationale: Live mint + probe with three concurrent lanes and a hit/miss matrix.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb describes the three lanes and matrix exactly as built.

edge_cases.rationale: Cross-site context is `synthetic` (postMessage faking) — real cross-site partitioning needs a different etld+1, which can only be approximated. Acknowledge this; also missing the revoke-then-fetch race.

openQuestions.title: Clarify the `synthetic cross-site` limitation
openQuestions.detail: Cross-site partitioning requires a different registrable domain, which a single-origin demo cannot replicate. Add a note explaining that the synthetic lane simulates the rule via a sandboxed iframe with an opaque origin, and link to the official test that uses two etld+1s.

openQuestions.title: Add a revoke-then-fetch lane
openQuestions.detail: URL.revokeObjectURL races are a common bug source. Add a lane that mints, fetches, revokes, fetches again — the second fetch should fail. Distinct from partition failure.

summary: Strong matrix view of the partitioning rule. The synthetic cross-site lane should be labelled as such to set expectations.
